program.title: Doctor of Veterinary Medicine
program.description: The College offers a six-year Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) program with 27 units of General Education (GE) courses taken in the College of Arts and Sciences, 21 units of foundation courses, and 173 units of core courses. The core courses are structured to provide an ever-increasing aptitude in the veterinary sciences to the students as they progress through the program. The core courses of the DVM program are offered by the three departments of the CVM, namely, the Department of Basic Veterinary Sciences, the Department of Veterinary Paraclinical Sciences, and the Department of Veterinary Clinical Sciences. The students acquire both theoretical knowledge and practical experience in animal production and in the diagnosis, prevention, treatment and control of diseases in companion, exotic and farm animals.
